segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In today's class I'm going to talk about marine archaeology, the branch of archaeology focusing on human interaction with the sea, lakes and rivers.
segments.zh: 今天的课上，我要讲的是海洋考古学，这是考古学的一个分支，主要研究人类与海洋、湖泊和河流的互动。它研究船只、货物、航运设施以及其他实物遗迹。我先给大家举个例子，然后展示这项研究如何通过最新技术的应用而发生变革。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's the study of ships, cargoes, shipping facilities, and other physical remains.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I'll give you an example, then go on to show how this type of research is being transformed by the use of the latest technology.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Atlit-Yam was a village on the coast of the eastern Mediterranean, which seems to have been thriving until around 7,000 BC.
segments.zh: 阿特利特-亚姆是东地中海沿岸的一个村庄，似乎一直繁荣到公元前7000年左右。居民们养牛、捕鱼、储存谷物。他们有淡水井，许多房屋围绕庭院建造，并用石头砌成。村里有一座令人印象深刻的纪念碑：七块半吨重的石头围绕着一处泉水排成半圆形，可能用于仪式目的。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The residents kept cattle, caught fish and stored grain.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They had wells for fresh water, many of their houses were built around a courtyard and were constructed of stone.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The village contained an impressive monument: seven half-tonne stones standing in a semicircle around a spring, that might have been used for ceremonial purposes.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Atlit-Yam may have been destroyed swiftly by a tsunami, or climate change may have caused glaciers to melt and sea levels to rise, flooding the village gradually.
segments.zh: 阿特利特-亚姆可能被海啸迅速摧毁，或者气候变化导致冰川融化和海平面上升，逐渐淹没了村庄。无论原因如何，它现在位于地中海海面下十米处，被埋在海底的沙子下。它被称为海底发现的最大、保存最完好的史前定居点。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Whatever the cause, it now lies ten metres below the surface of the Mediterranean, buried under sand at the bottom of the sea.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's been described as the largest and best preserved prehistoric settlement ever found on the seabed.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For marine archaeologists, Atlit-Yam is a treasure trove.
segments.zh: 对于海洋考古学家来说，阿特利特-亚姆是一个宝库。对建筑、工具和人类遗骸的研究揭示了这座繁忙村庄曾经如何运作，甚至一些居民患有哪些疾病。但当然，这只是一个村庄，一扇通往失落世界的窗户。为了更全面的图景，研究人员需要更多沉没的定居点，但困难在于找到它们。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Research on the buildings, tools and the human remains has revealed how the bustling village once functioned, and even what diseases some of its residents suffered from.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But of course this is only one small village, one window into a lost world.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For a fuller picture, researchers need more sunken settlements, but the hard part is finding them.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Underwater research used to require divers to find shipwrecks or artefacts, but in the second half of the twentieth century, various types of underwater vehicles were developed, some controlled from a ship on the surface, and some of them autonomous, which means they don't need to be operated by a person.
segments.zh: 水下研究过去需要潜水员寻找沉船或文物，但在20世纪下半叶，各种类型的水下航行器被开发出来，有些由水面上的船只控制，有些是自主的，这意味着它们不需要人来操作。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Autonomous underwater vehicles, or AUVs, are used in the oil industry, for instance, to create maps of the seabed before rigs and pipelines are installed.
segments.zh: 自主水下航行器（AUV）用于石油工业，例如在安装钻井平台和管道之前绘制海床地图。它们使用传感器（如指南针和声纳）进行导航。直到最近，它们还非常昂贵，而且很重，必须用绞车从大船上投放。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: To navigate they use sensors, such as compasses and sonar.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Until relatively recently they were very expensive, and so heavy that they had to be launched from a large vessel with a winch.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But the latest AUVs are much easier to manoeuvre – they can be launched from the shore or a small ship.
segments.zh: 但最新的AUV更容易操控——它们可以从岸边或小船上投放。而且它们便宜得多，这使得研究团队更容易获得。它们也非常先进，可以相互通信，例如，找出最有效的方式勘测一个地点，或在海床上找到特定物体。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And they're much cheaper, which makes them more accessible to research teams.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They're also very sophisticated.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They can communicate with each other and, for example, work out the most efficient way to survey a site, or to find particular objects on the seabed.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Field tests show the approach can work.
segments.zh: 现场测试表明这种方法可行。例如，在2015年的一次试验中，三架AUV在西西里海岸附近的马尔扎梅米搜寻沉船。该地点是一艘古罗马船只的最终安息地，这艘船在公元六世纪沉没，当时正在运送预制大理石构件用于建造一座早期教堂。AUV详细绘制了该区域的地图，发现了其他运载相同材料柱子的船只。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For example, in a trial in 2015, three AUVs searched for wrecks at Marzamemi, off the coast of Sicily.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The site is the final resting place of an ancient Roman ship, which sank in the sixth century AD while ferrying prefabricated marble elements for the construction of an early church.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The AUVs mapped the area in detail, finding other ships carrying columns of the same material.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Creating an internet in the sea for AUVs to communicate is no easy matter.
segments.zh: 为自主水下航行器在海底建立通信网络绝非易事。陆地上的WiFi网络使用电磁波，但在水中，电磁波只能传播几厘米。因此，需要更复杂的技术组合。短距离内，AUV可以利用光共享数据，而长距离通信则使用声波。不过，更具创意的解决方案也在开发中：在海底作业的AUV将数据卸载给第二个AUV，后者浮出水面并通过卫星将数据传回研究团队。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Wifi networks on land use electromagnetic waves, but in water these will only travel a few centimetres.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Instead, a more complex mix of technologies is required.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For short distances, AUVs can share data using light, while acoustic waves are used to communicate over long distances.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But more creative solutions are also being developed, where an AUV working on the seabed offloads data to a second AUV, which then surfaces and beams the data home to the research team using a satellite.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There's also a system that enables AUVs to share information from seabed scans, and other data.
segments.zh: 还有一种系统能让AUV共享海底扫描信息及其他数据。因此，如果一艘正在勘测海底的AUV发现了一个有趣的物体，它可以与附近携带更高级摄像头的AUV共享该物体的坐标（即位置），并安排那艘AUV对该物体进行更近距离的检查。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So if an AUV surveying the seabed finds an intriguing object, it can share the coordinates of the object – that is, its position – with a nearby AUV that carries superior cameras, and arrange for that AUV to make a closer inspection of the object.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Marine archaeologists are excited about the huge potential of these AUVs for their discipline.
segments.zh: 海洋考古学家对这些AUV在其学科中的巨大潜力感到兴奋。它们将被部署的一个地点是意大利海岸外的巴拉蒂湾。1974年，这里在18米深的水中发现了一艘有2000年历史的罗马船只。它沉没时，装载着医疗用品，装在木制或锡制容器中。这些货物让我们得以了解古时的治疗方法，包括一些被认为溶解后用于清洗眼睛的药片。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One site where they're going to be deployed is the Gulf of Baratti, off the Italian coast.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In 1974, a 2,000-year-old Roman vessel was discovered here, in 18 metres of water.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: When it sank, it was carrying medical goods, in wooden or tin receptacles.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Its cargo gives us insight into the treatments available all those years ago, including tablets that are thought to have been dissolved to form a cleansing liquid for the eyes.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Other Roman ships went down nearby, taking their cargoes with them.
segments.zh: 其他罗马船只也在附近沉没，连同它们的货物一起。有些船装载着巨大的赤陶罐。有些用于运输橄榄油，另一些则装载葡萄酒。在许多情况下，只有这些容器留存下来，而木船则被海底的淤泥掩埋。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Some held huge pots made of terracotta.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Some were used for transporting cargoes of olive oil, and others held wine.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In many cases it's only these containers that remain, while the wooden ships have been buried under silt on the seabed.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Another project that's about to ...
segments.zh: 另一个即将启动的项目……

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: coordinates of the object – that is, its position – with a nearby AUV that carries superior cameras, and arrange for that AUV to make a closer inspection of the object.
segments.zh: 与附近携带更高级摄像头的AUV共享该物体的坐标（即位置），并安排那艘AUV对该物体进行更近距离的检查。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Another project that's about to ...
segments.zh: 另一个即将启动的项目……

items.prompt:
The hunt for sunken settlements and ancient shipwrecks
ATLIT-YAM
• was a village on coast of eastern Mediterranean
• thrived until about 7,000 BC
• stone homes had a courtyard
• had a semicircle of large stones round a 31

pairs.q: semicircle of large stones round a
pairs.note: 原文中'semicircle around a spring'与题干'semicircle of large stones round a'对应，'spring'就是答案。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问的是半圆形大石头围绕什么，定位句直接说'stones standing in a semicircle around a spring'，所以答案就是spring。

items.prompt:
• cause of destruction unknown – now under the sea
• biggest settlement from the prehistoric period found on the seabed
• research carried out into structures, 32

pairs.p: buildings, tools and the human remains
pairs.note: 题干中的'structures'在原文中被具体化为'buildings, tools and the human remains'，其中'tools'是答案。

paraphrase.explain: 题干提到'research carried out into structures'，原文对应'Research on the buildings, tools and the human remains'，其中'tools'是答案。

items.prompt:
TRADITIONAL AUTONOMOUS UNDERWATER VEHICLES (AUVs)
• used in the oil industry, e.g. to make 33

pairs.note: make 和 create 是同义词，都表示'制作'。
答案 maps 直接出现在定位句中，与题号 33 对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问传统AUV在石油工业中用来制作什么，定位句说'to create maps of the seabed'，所以答案就是maps。

pairs.note: 题干和原文中完全相同的词，直接对应。
题干中的'problems'概括了原文中'expensive'和'heavy'两个问题，这里'heavy'是具体问题之一，通过'and so heavy that'引出。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到AUVs过去很贵且很重，题干问的是它们的问题，其中一个是昂贵，另一个是重，所以答案就是'heavy'。

items.prompt:
Tests:
• Marzamemi, Sicily: found ancient Roman ships carrying architectural elements made of 35

pairs.note: 'architectural elements' 对应 'prefabricated marble elements'，'made of' 隐含在 'marble' 中，表示材质。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'prefabricated marble elements'，其中 'marble' 就是题干中 'architectural elements made of' 的材质，因此答案就是 marble。

items.prompt:
Underwater internet:
• 36 is used for short distance communication, acoustic waves for long distance

pairs.note: 题干用'communication'，原文用'distances'，但意思相同，都是指短距离传输。
题干和原文完全一致，直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问短距离通信使用什么，原文在'For short distances'后直接提到'using light'，因此定位到light。

items.prompt:
• plans for communication with researchers by satellite
• AUV can send data to another AUV that has better 37

pairs.note: better 和 superior 是同义词，都表示'更好的'。
原文直接出现 cameras，与答案一致。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 AUV 可以共享坐标给 nearby AUV that carries superior cameras，其中 superior 对应题干 better，cameras 即为答案。

items.prompt:
Planned research in Gulf of Baratti:
• to find out more about wrecks of ancient Roman ships, including
– one carrying 38 supplies; tablets may have been used for cleaning the 39

pairs.note: 题干中的'supplies'对应原文中的'goods'，都是指货物。
题干中的'cleaning'对应原文中的'cleansing'，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位到原文中'carrying medical goods'，然后根据'cleansing liquid for the eyes'找到答案'medical'。

items.prompt: – one carrying 38 supplies; tablets may have been used for cleaning the 39

pairs.note: 'cleaning' 替换为 'cleansing liquid for'，'eyes' 是答案。
'supplies' 替换为 'goods'，'medical' 对应 '38' 的答案。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问 'cleaning the 39'，定位句说 'cleansing liquid for the eyes'，所以 39 是 eyes。

items.prompt: – others carrying containers of olive oil or 40

pairs.p: olive oil, and others held wine
pairs.note: 题干中'olive oil or 40'对应原文'olive oil, and others held wine'，其中'40'是题号，实际答案词是'wine'。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'Some were used for transporting cargoes of olive oil, and others held wine'，其中'others'对应题干中的'others carrying containers of olive oil or'，后面直接给出答案'wine'。
